64.reason: [Using post-increment (high++) passes the current value of high to the recursive call and then increments it locally, which does not correctly propagate increased height to the right subtree. Should use high+1 instead.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.5 , reason:  [Using post-increment (high++) passes the current value of high to the recursive call and then increments it locally, which does not correctly propagate increased height to the right subtree. Should use high+1 instead.]

65.reason: [Same issue as above: high++ modifies a local copy and doesn't reflect the correct height for the right subtree. Also, after the first recursive call, high may have been incremented, leading to incorrect height for the right subtree.]
